children.name: Eupetes
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the Malaysian Rail-babbler, one of the most enigmatic passerines in Southeast Asia. It has an exceptionally long neck, long legs, and a strikingly long tail, giving it a superficial resemblance to a rail. It is a skulking, ground-dwelling bird of the remote lowland forest floor, with a distinctive, far-carrying call that is far more frequently heard than the bird is seen.

children.name: Eupetes macrocerus
children.description: A bizarre, rail-like bird with a very long neck, a long tail, and brown-and-black striped upperparts that echo the undergrowth, found in the lowland rainforests of the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, and Borneo. It is a secretive, terrestrial species that walks like a rail along the forest floor, searching for insects and small invertebrates. Its extraordinary, loud, three-noted whistle has earned it mystical reverence among local forest dwellers.
